This field is always set.

List of UUIDs of the objects in the group.
Only populated if show_aggregation_uuids is set.

aggregation_value.description: Value of the aggregation operation as calculated by the query.

Map of counts for the given unique_count_paths fields.
Only populated if unique_count_paths is set.

unique_values.description:
Map of values for the given unique_value_paths fields.
Only populated if unique_value_paths is set.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

app_password.description: The App Password associated with the Bitbucket Cloud account.

username.description: The username of the Bitbucket Cloud account used for authentication.

personal_access_token.description: PAT of the user doing integration to fetch resources using REST APIs.

The access token associated with the Bitbucket Cloud account.
https://support.atlassian.com/bitbucket-cloud/docs/access-tokens

enable_full_scan is a boolean value that indicates if the full scan should
be enabled.

comments should be enabled.

are enabled.

host_url.description: The URL of the workspace (only for cloud) or project.

webhook_secret.description:
The webhook secret configured in Bitbucket for validating incoming webhook
events.

be enabled.

enable_pr_comments is a boolean value that indicates if the automated PR
comments should be enabled.

enable_pr_scans.description:

are enabled.

repos should be included. Default will be off.

be enabled.

comments should be enabled.

are enabled.

The webhook secret configured in GitLab for validating incoming webhook
events.

GroupAggregationValueResponse returns the value of the aggregation if
requested.

v1GroupResponse.description: Response to a list group request.

v1IndexData.description:
IndexData is used to index the resource for search. It's an internal
object.

search_score.description: search_score is the score of the resource for search. Internal use only.

will_be_deleted_at.description: Time that the resource will be deleted.

v1Installation.description: Installation represents an installation request in the system.

meta.description: Metadata is generic for any object.

propagate.description: Propagate indicates that the object should be visible in child namespaces.

tenant_meta.description: Namespaces are associated with a tenant.

cleanup_stale_namespaces.description:
Indicates whether stale managed namespaces that no longer correspond
to groups or projects in the SCM should be automatically deleted
during installation reconciliation. Default value is false.

enabled_features.title: Enabled features. The valid values are "git, github"

external_id.description: The external ID of the installation.

external_name.description: The external name of the installation.

include_archived_repos.description:
include_archived_repos is a boolean value that indicates if the archived
repos should be included. Default will be off.

ingestion_time.description: The last time that we ingested the installation data.

ingestion_token.description:
The API token that the scanner can use for scanning installation
related information. If it is empty, the scanner will use local
authentication information.

installation_error_message.description:
Message explaining why the installation is invalid.
This field is populated when invalid is true.

invalid.description:
Identifies the installations that are in the system,
but potentially their corresponding configuration has been removed
from GitHub and thus we cannot authenticate. In this case,
we stop trying to re-sync with this installation.

login.description:
The login of the account taken directly from the GitHub response
VS spec.user, which is inferred from the identity token in the request
context.

marked_for_deletion.description:
Indicates the installation is marked for deletion,
and in next sync call the installation with its related data will be
deleted.

platform_type.description:
The platform type for the installation, which can be GitHub, GitLab,
Azure, or Bitbucket.

project_uuids.description: The list of projects that are associated with this installation.

public.description: Apply only to public repositories. Default value is false.

scm_app_uuid.description: The UUID of the SCM app being installed.

suspended.description: Indicates if the installation is suspended.

target_type.description: The target of the installation (Organization or User).

user.description:
The user name of the user that initiated the installation.
Refers back to the GitHub user name.

v1ListInstallationsResponse.description: Response to the request to list installations.

next_page_id.description: The UUID of last message retrieved.

next_page_token.description: Pagination token that can be used to retrieve the next page of results.

v1Meta.description: Common fields for all Endor Labs resources.

annotations.description:
Annotations can be used to attach metadata to a resource message.
Annotation values can be small or large, structured or unstructured,
and may include characters not permitted by labels.
The keys may contain alphanumerics, underscores (_), dots (.) and dashes
(-). The values of an annotation must be 16384 bytes or smaller.

create_time.description:
Time the resource was created.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

created_by.description:
Name and authentication source of the user who created the object,
for example, ewok@endor.ai@google@api-key.

description.description: Resource description. Must be less than 1024 bytes.

kind.description:
Resource kind, for example, HelloResponse.
Auto-generated using the protobuf message proto.MessageName().Name().

name.description: Resource name. Must be 63 characters or less.

parent_kind.description: Parent object resource kind, for example, Project.

references.description: Map of objects referenced in a query API.

tags.description:
List of tags attached to the resource.
Tags can be used to select objects and to find collections of objects that
satisfy certain conditions. A tag must be 255 characters or less.

update_time.description:
Time the resource was last updated.
Note: Updated on all create/patch/delete operations.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

updated_by.description:
Name and authentication source of the last user who updated the object,
for example, vulnerabilityingestor@endor.ai@x509.

upsert_time.description:
Time the resource was last upserted.

Note:
create_time is only set the first time the resource is created.
upsert_time is set every time the resource is upseted.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

v1PlatformSource.description: Type of source control platform a resource was discovered on.

analytic_time.description:
Last time a project was analyzed.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

disable_automated_scan.description:
This is a private package and it must not be scanned by the background
scheduler. It will be scanned by an endorctl client instead. Default:
false.

queue_time.description:
Last time a project was queued.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

scan_time.description:
Last time a project was ingested.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt

full_history_scan_time.title: Last time a project was scanned with deep secrets scanning

v1ScanState.description:
Scan state for a project or package version.

 - SCAN_STATE_NOT_PROCESSED: Object has not been processed by the system yet.
Set when the project or package is created for the first time.
A new project or package version is automatically placed in this state.
 - SCAN_STATE_IDLE: Object has been scanned at least once.
 - SCAN_STATE_INGESTING: Object is being scanned.
 - SCAN_STATE_ANALYTIC: Object is being analyzed.
 - SCAN_STATE_UNREACHABLE: Object cannot be ingested because it is not reachable from the scheduler.
 - SCAN_STATE_REQUEST_FULL_RESCAN: Object is marked for a complete rescan.
This only applies to OSS projects.
 - SCAN_STATE_REQUEST_INCREMENTAL_RESCAN: Object is marked for an incremental rescan, where only new packages
discovered in the scan are added. Indicates that this project should be
rescanned with a higher priority. Often because it is used by a customer
and we need any new packages to be discovered and scanned with higher
priority. If the project is put in this state, we will scan any new
packages at high priority but we will not re-scan older packages.
 - SCAN_STATE_QUEUED: Object is queued to be scanned.
 - SCAN_STATE_QUARANTINED: Scan scheduling was unsuccessful due to a system errors. Object is
quarantined from additional scheduling. Potentially object is an
orphan or similar situations.

v1TenantMeta.description: Tenant related data for the tenant containing the resource.

namespace.description:
Namespaces are a way to organize organizational units into virtual
groupings of resources. Namespaces must be a fully qualified name,
for example, the child namespace of namespace "endor.prod" called "app"
is called "endor.prod.app".

v1UpdateRequest.description: Message used for all update requests.

force.description:
Force will force the update of the resource if any
checks fail.

update_mask.description: Fields to update. Defaults to all fields.
